title: While Loops
beginnerExplanation: While loops repeat a block of code as long as a condition remains true. They're ideal when you don't know in advance how many times to repeat.
intermediateExplanation: Sometimes you need to keep running code until something happens — like a game loop that runs until the player quits, or a password prompt that repeats until the correct one is entered.
advancedExplanation: while condition:\n    # code to repeat\n    # update condition

realWorldAnalogy.title: Waiting for a Bus
realWorldAnalogy.description: You stand at the stop and keep checking: "Has the bus arrived?" If no, you wait and check again. When it arrives (condition becomes False), you stop waiting and board.

mentalModel: You stand at the stop and keep checking: "Has the bus arrived?" If no, you wait and check again. When it arrives (condition becomes False), you stop waiting and board.

executionWalkthrough: 2. Scopes and local namespaces are verified.
3. Target variables are allocated or updated.
commonMistakes: Creating an infinite loop by forgetting to update the condition variable.
Using = instead of == in the condition (assignment vs comparison).
Off-by-one errors — loop runs one too many or too few times.

debuggingMission.scenario: An adventurer is trying to use While Loops but hit a common pitfall: Creating an infinite loop by forgetting to update the condition variable.
debuggingMission.brokenCode: while condition:\n    # code to repeat\n    # update condition
debuggingMission.explanation: Make sure to follow the best practices: Always ensure the condition will eventually become False (avoid infinite loops).

practiceQuestions.question: When does a while loop stop executing?
options: After a fixed number of iterations
When it reaches the end of the file
practiceQuestions.explanation: A while loop checks its condition before each iteration. When the condition evaluates to False, the loop exits.

practiceQuestions.question: What does `break` do inside a while loop?
options: Restarts the loop from the beginning
practiceQuestions.explanation: break immediately terminates the innermost loop, regardless of the condition.

developerMode.industryUsage: While Loops is widely used in standard library scripts and enterprise Python programs.
developerMode.performanceConsiderations: Optimized by standard compiler rules in modern Python environments.
bestPractices: Always ensure the condition will eventually become False (avoid infinite loops).
Update the loop variable inside the loop body.
Use break to exit early and continue to skip to the next iteration.
antiPatterns: Creating an infinite loop by forgetting to update the condition variable.
Using = instead of == in the condition (assignment vs comparison).
Off-by-one errors — loop runs one too many or too few times.
